goal: close the two highest-priority blockers by swapping current entry and canonical shell files to the prepared drafts

blockers.description: The repository root default page still does not collapse into the canonical contract-driven entry.
The canonical shell still does not default to the prepared dropzone-first asset resolution logic.

swapSequence.reason: Collapse the root default page into a canonical handoff to web/reference-shell/index.contract.html instead of leaving old and new entry surfaces in parallel.
Adopt canonical dropzone-first asset resolution in the contract-driven shell while preserving truth-boundary disclosure.
Make the canonical contract-driven shell actually point to the dropzone-ready implementation draft.

postSwapChecks: Opening the web root no longer leaves the operator in the old execution shell as a parallel default entry.
The canonical shell continues to display scene status, evidence grade, anchor boundary, allowed mode and risk disclosures.
scene_03_structure_primary.mp4 resolves automatically from web/assets/first-wave/ when present.
scene_06_conservation_boundary.mp4 resolves automatically from web/assets/first-wave/ when present.
scene_01_orientation_order.mp4 resolves automatically from web/assets/first-wave/ when present.
Poster fallback works when video is absent.
scene_00_intro_entry remains exploratory/disclosure-first even if files exist.
Manifest remains a fact/supplement layer rather than the user's required wiring table.
notes: This patchset does not claim the swaps are already applied.
It exists to eliminate ambiguity about which files should now be replaced and in what order.
Until these swaps are actually applied to the current live files, the blockers remain open.
